1â†’# HW04 - Paint & Visitor Ballworld: Command Pattern
     2â†’
     3â†’## Design Pattern
     4â†’
     5â†’This assignment introduces the **Command Pattern** (via `IBallCmd`) and the
     6â†’**IPaintStrategy** abstraction. The dispatcher now sends `IBallCmd` commands
     7â†’rather than raw `Graphics` objects, enabling decoupled command-based updates.
     8â†’Additionally, ball visual appearance is separated from behavior via
     9â†’`IPaintStrategy` and its abstract base `APaintStrategy`, which uses
    10â†’`AffineTransform` for resolution-independent rendering.
    11â†’
    12â†’## Architecture Overview
    13â†’
    14â†’```
    15â†’Controller
    16â†’â”śâ”€â”€ BallModel (model)
    17â†’â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ IDispatcher<IBallCmd> - dispatches commands (not Graphics)
    18â†’â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ Ball instances (implement IObserver<IBallCmd>, IBall)
    19â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ IUpdateStrategy - movement/behavior (from HW03)
    20â†’â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ IPaintStrategy - visual appearance (new)
    21â†’â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ Strategy loading via reflection + factories
    22â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ Paint strategy loading via reflection + factories
    23â†’â””â”€â”€ BallView (view)
    24â†’    â”śâ”€â”€ Update strategy selector
    25â†’    â”śâ”€â”€ Paint strategy selector
    26â†’    â””â”€â”€ Canvas panel
    27â†’```
    28â†’
    29â†’**Key changes from HW03**:
    30â†’- `Ball` now implements `IBall` interface (not just `IObserver<Graphics>`)
    31â†’- Dispatcher type changes from `IDispatcher<Graphics>` to `IDispatcher<IBallCmd>`
    32â†’- `Ball.update()` receives an `IBallCmd` and calls `cmd.apply(this, disp)`
    33â†’- `IBall.execute(IBallAlgo)` supports the Visitor pattern (calls `algo.caseDefault`)
    34â†’- New `IPaintStrategy` / `APaintStrategy` for visual rendering
    35â†’
    36â†’## What Is Provided
    37â†’
    38â†’- **`IBall.java`** (`src/hw04/model/IBall.java`): Interface with getters/setters
    39â†’  for location, velocity, radius, color, dimension, updateStrategy, paintStrategy,
    40â†’  canvas, plus `move()`, `bounce()`, `paint()`, `execute()`, `updateState()`.
    41â†’- **`IBallCmd.java`** (`src/hw04/model/IBallCmd.java`): Command interface with
    42â†’  `apply(IBall context, IDispatcher<IBallCmd> disp)`.
    43â†’- **`IUpdateStrategy.java`** (`src/hw04/model/IUpdateStrategy.java`): Strategy
    44â†’  interface with `updateState(IBall context, IDispatcher<IBallCmd> disp)` and
    45â†’  `init(IBall context)`. Includes `NULL` and `errorStrategy` static instances.
    46â†’- **`IPaintStrategy.java`** (`src/hw04/model/paint/strategies/IPaintStrategy.java`):
    47â†’  Paint strategy interface with `init(IBall ball)` and `paint(Graphics g, IBall ball)`.
    48â†’  Includes `NULL` and `ERROR` static instances.
    49â†’- **`APaintStrategy.java`** (`src/hw04/model/paint/strategies/APaintStrategy.java`):
    50â†’  Abstract paint strategy using `AffineTransform`. The `paint()` method sets up
    51â†’  translate/scale/rotate transforms and calls `paintCfg()` then `paintXfrm()`.
    52â†’- **`ShapePaintStrategy.java`** (`src/hw04/model/paint/strategies/ShapePaintStrategy.java`):
    53â†’  Concrete strategy that paints a `java.awt.Shape` using the affine transform.
    54â†’- **`Ball.java`** (`src/hw04/model/Ball.java`): Concrete ball implementing `IBall`.
    55â†’  The `update()` method calls `cmd.apply(this, disp)`. The `paint()` method
    56â†’  delegates to `paintStrategy.paint(g, this)`. The `execute()` method calls
    57â†’  `algo.caseDefault(this)`.
    58â†’- **`BallModel.java`** (`src/hw04/model/BallModel.java`): Model with update
    59â†’  strategy and paint strategy factory loading.
    60â†’- **Shape factories** (`src/hw04/model/paint/shape/`): `EllipseFac`, `RectangleFac`,
    61â†’  `PolygonFac`, etc. for creating geometric shapes.
    62â†’- **Controller, View**: Fully implemented MVC with dual strategy dropdowns.
    63â†’
    64â†’## What You Implement
    65â†’
    66â†’### Update Strategies (`src/hw04/model/strategy/`)
    67â†’
    68â†’All update strategies implement `IUpdateStrategy` with
    69â†’`updateState(IBall context, IDispatcher<IBallCmd> disp)` and `init(IBall context)`.
    70â†’
    71â†’#### 1. `StraightStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw04/model/strategy/StraightStrategy.java`
    72â†’No-op. Ball moves straight using default `move()` and `bounce()`.
    73â†’
    74â†’#### 2. `GravityStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw04/model/strategy/GravityStrategy.java`
    75â†’Add a constant (e.g., 1) to `context.getVelocity().y` each tick.
    76â†’
    77â†’#### 3. `ColorStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw04/model/strategy/ColorStrategy.java`
    78â†’Change the ball's color to a random color each tick.
    79â†’
    80â†’#### 4. `BreathingStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw04/model/strategy/BreathingStrategy.java`
    81â†’Oscillate the ball's radius using the `sineMaker` (SineMaker) field. Each tick:
    82â†’```java
    83â†’context.setRadius(sineMaker.getDVal());
    84â†’```
    85â†’
    86â†’#### 5. `SwitcherStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw04/model/strategy/SwitcherStrategy.java`
    87â†’Delegate to the internal strategy (same pattern as HW03, adapted for the new
    88â†’`IBall`/`IBallCmd` signatures).
    89â†’
    90â†’#### 6. `MultiStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw04/model/strategy/MultiStrategy.java`
    91â†’Execute both `firstStrategy` and `secondStrategy` on the context ball:
    92â†’```java
    93â†’firstStrategy.updateState(context, disp);
    94â†’secondStrategy.updateState(context, disp);
    95â†’```
    96â†’
    97â†’### Paint Strategies (`src/hw04/model/paint/strategies/`)
    98â†’
    99â†’#### 7. `EllipsePaintStrategy` -- `src/hw04/model/paint/strategies/EllipsePaintStrategy.java`
   100â†’Extends `ShapePaintStrategy`. Constructor creates an `Ellipse2D.Double` shape
   101â†’at unit coordinates (e.g., `(-1, -1, 2, 2)`) that gets scaled by the affine
   102â†’transform in `APaintStrategy.paint()`.
   103â†’
   104â†’#### 8. `SquarePaintStrategy` -- `src/hw04/model/paint/strategies/SquarePaintStrategy.java`
   105â†’Implements `IPaintStrategy` directly (non-affine-transform-based). Paint a filled
   106â†’square centered at the ball's location using `g.fillRect()` with the ball's
   107â†’radius as half-width.
   108â†’
   109â†’#### 9. `MultiPaintStrategy` -- `src/hw04/model/paint/strategies/MultiPaintStrategy.java`
   110â†’Extends `APaintStrategy`. Holds an array of `APaintStrategy` sub-strategies.
   111â†’- `init()`: Call `init()` on each sub-strategy
   112â†’- `paintCfg()`: Call `paintCfg()` on each sub-strategy
   113â†’- `paintXfrm()`: Call `paintXfrm()` on each sub-strategy with the same transform
   114â†’
   115â†’## File Paths (Editable)
   116â†’
   117â†’| File | Package | What to implement |
   118â†’|------|---------|-------------------|
   119â†’| `src/hw04/model/strategy/StraightStrategy.java` | `hw04.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   120â†’| `src/hw04/model/strategy/GravityStrategy.java` | `hw04.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   121â†’| `src/hw04/model/strategy/ColorStrategy.java` | `hw04.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   122â†’| `src/hw04/model/strategy/BreathingStrategy.java` | `hw04.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   123â†’| `src/hw04/model/strategy/SwitcherStrategy.java` | `hw04.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   124â†’| `src/hw04/model/strategy/MultiStrategy.java` | `hw04.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   125â†’| `src/hw04/model/paint/strategies/EllipsePaintStrategy.java` | `hw04.model.paint.strategies` | constructors |
   126â†’| `src/hw04/model/paint/strategies/SquarePaintStrategy.java` | `hw04.model.paint.strategies` | `init()`, `paint()` |
   127â†’| `src/hw04/model/paint/strategies/MultiPaintStrategy.java` | `hw04.model.paint.strategies` | `init()`, `paintCfg()`, `paintXfrm()` |
   128â†’
   129â†’## Expected Behavior
   130â†’
   131â†’- **Command dispatch**: The model creates an `IBallCmd` each tick that calls
   132â†’  `updateState()`, `move()`, `bounce()`, and `paint()` on each ball. This is
   133â†’  dispatched to all balls via `dispatcher.updateAll(cmd)`.
   134â†’- **Update strategies**: Same behavioral effects as HW03 (gravity, color change,
   135â†’  breathing, etc.) but now through the `IBall`/`IBallCmd` interface.
   136â†’- **Paint strategies**: Determine the visual shape of each ball:
   137â†’  - `EllipsePaintStrategy`: Draws an ellipse scaled/rotated by the affine transform
   138â†’  - `SquarePaintStrategy`: Draws a filled square at the ball's position
   139â†’  - `MultiPaintStrategy`: Combines multiple paint strategies (e.g., ellipse + pentangle)
   140â†’- **Dual strategy selection**: GUI allows selecting both an update strategy and a
   141â†’  paint strategy for each new ball.
   142â†’
   143â†’## Dependency on HW03
   144â†’
   145â†’This assignment builds on HW03 (depends_on: `comp310_hw03`). Key differences:
   146â†’- Dispatcher type changes from `Graphics` to `IBallCmd`
   147â†’- `IBall` interface replaces concrete `Ball` reference in strategies
   148â†’- New `IPaintStrategy`/`APaintStrategy` layer for rendering
   149â†’- `Ball.update()` now receives commands, not graphics directly
   150â†’
   151â†’## Testing
   152â†’
   153â†’Run the autograder to validate your implementation:
   154â†’
   155â†’```
   156â†’Use the `grade` tool to run tests.
   157â†’```
   158â†’
   159â†’The test suite (`comp310.tests.hw04.PaintStrategyTest`) verifies:
   160â†’- Update strategies correctly modify ball state via the `IBall` interface
   161â†’- Paint strategies render correctly
   162â†’- `MultiStrategy` composes two update strategies
   163â†’- `MultiPaintStrategy` composes multiple paint strategies
   164â†’- Affine-transform-based paint strategies scale/rotate properly
   165â†’

## Summary: ISineMaker Interface and SineMaker Implementation

I've found the complete ISineMaker interface and SineMaker implementation in the COMP 310 provided library. Here's what ISineMaker exposes:

### ISineMaker Interface
**Location:** `/Users/bebe/Code/bscs-bench/comp310/lib/provided/utils/valueGenerator/ISineMaker.java`

**Methods:**

1. **`double getDblVal()`**
   - Returns the next sinusoidal value as a double
   - Values vary smoothly in a sinusoidal fashion
   - Increments internally by the delta angle each call

2. **`int getIntVal()`**
   - Returns the next sinusoidal value rounded to the nearest integer
   - Simply calls `getDblVal()` internally and rounds the result
   - Note: `getDblVal()` and `getIntVal()` are not independentâ€”calling `getIntVal()` also advances the internal state

### SineMaker Implementation
**Location:** `/Users/bebe/Code/bscs-bench/comp310/lib/provided/utils/valueGenerator/impl/SineMaker.java`

**Constructor:**
```java
public SineMaker(double min, double max, double delta)
```
- **min** - The minimum and initial value to produce
- **max** - The maximum value to produce  
- **delta** - The amount in radians that the generating angle is incremented each time

**Usage Example (from HW4):**
```java
ISineMaker sineMaker = new SineMaker(5, 20, 0.2);
```
This creates a sine maker that oscillates between 5 and 20, incrementing the angle by 0.2 radians each call.

**Internal Formula:**
- Initializes theta to `-Ď€/2` (to start at minimum value)
- Each call: `result = mid + amplitude Ă— sin(theta)` where:
  - `mid = (max + min) / 2`
  - `amplitude = (max - min) / 2`
  - `theta` increments by `delta` each call

1â†’#!/bin/bash
     2â†’# COMP 310 Build & Test Script
     3â†’# Compiles student code against provided.jar, compiles tests, runs JUnit 5.
     4â†’#
     5â†’# Usage: build.sh <hwNN> [workspace_path]
     6â†’#   $1 - assignment name (hw01, hw02, ..., hw06)
     7â†’#   $2 - workspace mount path (default: /workspace)
     8â†’
     9â†’set -euo pipefail
    10â†’
    11â†’HW=${1:?Usage: build.sh <hwNN> [workspace_path]}
    12â†’WORKSPACE=${2:-/workspace}
    13â†’BUILD_DIR=/tmp/build
    14â†’REPORTS_DIR=$WORKSPACE/test-reports
    15â†’
    16â†’# Classpath components
    17â†’PROVIDED_JAR=/opt/comp310/provided.jar
    18â†’JUNIT_DIR=/opt/comp310/junit5
    19â†’JUNIT_STANDALONE=$JUNIT_DIR/junit-platform-console-standalone-1.10.2.jar
    20â†’
    21â†’# â”€â”€ Clean â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
    22â†’rm -rf "$BUILD_DIR" "$REPORTS_DIR"
    23â†’mkdir -p "$BUILD_DIR" "$REPORTS_DIR"
    24â†’
    25â†’# â”€â”€ 1. Compile student code against provided.jar â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
    26â†’STUDENT_SRCS=$(find "$WORKSPACE/src" -name "*.java" ! -name "module-info.java" 2>/dev/null || true)
    27â†’if [ -z "$STUDENT_SRCS" ]; then
    28â†’    echo "ERROR: No Java source files found in $WORKSPACE/src" >&2
    29â†’    exit 1
    30â†’fi
    31â†’
    32â†’echo "==> Compiling student source files..."
    33â†’javac -cp "$PROVIDED_JAR" \
    34â†’      -d "$BUILD_DIR" \
    35â†’      $STUDENT_SRCS 2>&1
    36â†’
    37â†’echo "==> Student code compiled successfully."
    38â†’
    39â†’# â”€â”€ 2. Compile test sources against student code + provided + JUnit â”€â”€
    40â†’TEST_ROOT=${COMP310_TESTS_DIR:-/opt/comp310/tests}
    41â†’TEST_DIR=$TEST_ROOT/$HW
    42â†’if [ -d "$TEST_DIR" ]; then
    43â†’    TEST_SRCS=$(find "$TEST_DIR" -name "*.java" 2>/dev/null || true)
    44â†’    if [ -n "$TEST_SRCS" ]; then
    45â†’        echo "==> Compiling test files from $TEST_DIR..."
    46â†’        javac -cp "$PROVIDED_JAR:$BUILD_DIR:$JUNIT_DIR/*" \
    47â†’              -d "$BUILD_DIR" \
    48â†’              $TEST_SRCS 2>&1
    49â†’        echo "==> Tests compiled successfully."
    50â†’    else
    51â†’        echo "WARNING: No test source files found in $TEST_DIR" >&2
    52â†’    fi
    53â†’else
    54â†’    echo "WARNING: Test directory $TEST_DIR does not exist" >&2
    55â†’fi
    56â†’
    57â†’# â”€â”€ 3. Run JUnit 5 tests â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
    58â†’echo "==> Running JUnit 5 tests..."
    59â†’set +e
    60â†’java -jar "$JUNIT_STANDALONE" \
    61â†’     --class-path "$BUILD_DIR:$PROVIDED_JAR" \
    62â†’     --scan-classpath \
    63â†’     --reports-dir "$REPORTS_DIR" \
    64â†’     --fail-if-no-tests \
    65â†’     2>&1
    66â†’TEST_EXIT=$?
    67â†’set -e
    68â†’
    69â†’# â”€â”€ 4. Optional fat JAR (skipped by default, set BUILD_JAR=1 to enable)
    70â†’if [ "${BUILD_JAR:-0}" = "1" ]; then
    71â†’    FAT_JAR=$WORKSPACE/submission.jar
    72â†’    echo "==> Building submission JAR..."
    73â†’    mkdir -p /tmp/fatjar
    74â†’    cp -r "$BUILD_DIR"/* /tmp/fatjar/ 2>/dev/null || true
    75â†’    cd /tmp/fatjar
    76â†’    jar xf "$PROVIDED_JAR" 2>/dev/null || true
    77â†’    jar cf "$FAT_JAR" -C /tmp/fatjar . 2>/dev/null && \
    78â†’        echo "==> Submission JAR created: $FAT_JAR" || \
    79â†’        echo "WARNING: Failed to create submission JAR (non-fatal)" >&2
    80â†’    rm -rf /tmp/fatjar
    81â†’fi
    82â†’
    83â†’exit $TEST_EXIT
    84â†’
